# APCP — Construction Project Control Platform

Axion Imagineering's internal project-control app. React (Vite) SPA + Supabase (Postgres + Auth), no custom backend/Edge Functions.

## Core conventions

**Project is root.** Every operational record is scoped by `project_code` (e.g. `SCB`), read from `activeProject` via `useProject()`. Always use `activeProject.project_code` / `activeProject.project_name` — **not** `.code` / `.name` (see Known Issues below).

**Numbering.** Document numbers are generated by `genDocNumber(typeKey, projectCode, sequence)` in `src/config/docTypes.js` — format `TYPE-PROJCODE-YEAR-SEQUENCE`. Don't hand-roll numbering elsewhere.

**Services own data access.** Each domain has a `src/services/xService.js` with plain async methods (`list`, `get`, `create`, `update`) wrapping a single Supabase table — see `supplierService.js` for the canonical shape. Pages call services; they don't inline raw Supabase queries for CRUD.

**"Register" pattern (MAR, RFI, Shop Drawing, IR).** These are read-only trackers, auto-populated from their corresponding IF-form — never a separate manually-entered log:

| Register | Source form | Table |
|---|---|---|
| MAR Register (`/mar`) | IF05 — Material Approval Certificate (`/mac`) | `if05` |
| RFI Register (`/rfi-register`) | IF08 — Request For Information (`/rfi`) | `if08` |
| Shop Drawing Register (`/sd-register`) | IF04 — Shop Drawing Submittal (`/shop-drawings`) | `if04` |
| IR Register (`/ir-register`) | IF09 — Activity Inspection Request (`/ir`) | `if09` |

Each register's page component (`src/pages/{mar,rfi,sd,ir}/*Register.jsx`) has **no** create/edit/delete UI — it reads the IF-table directly, derives a display status client-side (never stored), and renders + exports its own A3-landscape PDF. Status derivation:
- MAR / Shop Drawing: from the form's existing `response_code` (`A —`/`B —`/`C —`/`D —` prefix) + `status`, mapped to `Pending / Under Review / Approved / Approved with Comments / Revised and Resubmit / Rejected`.
- RFI: from `required_response_date` vs `response_date` → `Under Review / Replied On-Time / Replied Late / Overdue / Cancelled`. No fixed turnaround constant — each RFI's own required date decides it.
- IR: from `result` (`Passed/Conditional Pass/Failed`) mapped onto the same A/B/C/D/UR scheme as MAR/SD, plus a separate computed `Overdue` flag (fixed 7-day turnaround from `date`/`requested_inspection_date`, since IF09 and IF04 have no per-record required-date field). Overdue is layered on top of "Under Review," never a 6th lettered code.

If you build a new register for another IF-form, follow this exact pattern — read-only, status derived not stored, same print/header convention below.

**Print convention (all registers + printEngine.js forms).** A3 landscape, three-party header (Client / Employer — Contractor (Axion, hardcoded logo) — Consultant), each cell showing logo-or-name-fallback via a shared `logoCell()` helper. Below that: Log Info (left) → Register Summary (center) → Status Legend (right), in that exact order. `src/utils/printEngine.js` holds the shared `buildHeader()` / `mergeProjectLogos()` used by IF04–IF12 + DAR; the four Register pages have their own near-identical `exportPDF()` since they're a different document shape. Axion's own logo is `AXION_LOGO` from `src/utils/axionLogo.js` — never admin-uploadable, always hardcoded. Client/Consultant logos come from `projects.client_logo` / `projects.consultant_logo` (base64, uploaded via Project Register → Parties & Logos).

**People & Access Management** lives entirely under the existing "Roles & Permissions" sidebar entry (`/roles` → `src/pages/admin/RolesPermissions.jsx`) — deliberately *not* a separate "People & Access" nav section. Six internal tabs: Roles & Permissions (matrix), Employee Register, User Accounts, Project Assignments, Digital Signatures, Cost Allocation (placeholder only). Key architectural decisions:
- **No service_role key / Edge Functions anywhere in this app.** Account creation is self-service: an Admin creates an `employees` row (with email); the employee visits `/signup`, and `userService.signUp()` only succeeds if their email matches an *Active* employees row. New accounts default to Viewer; Admin upgrades the role afterward via User Accounts. Forgotten passwords use Supabase's own `resetPasswordForEmail` — never a custom temp-password/hash scheme.
- **User Accounts extends `profiles`** (1:1 with `auth.users`) rather than a separate `app_users` table — `profiles` already was "a person with app access," so the new fields (`employee_id`, `account_status`, `last_login`, `email`) were added onto it.
- Employees are **never hard-deleted** — `employeeService.deactivate()` only flips `status` to Inactive.
- New services: `employeeService`, `userService`, `roleService`, `permissionService`, `projectAssignmentService`, `signatureService`. Config catalog (role list, permission modules/actions) lives in `src/config/peopleAccessConfig.js`.

## Known issues / deferred work

- **`activeProject.code` / `.name` bug** — the `projects` table only has `project_code` / `project_name`, no `code`/`name` columns. `MARRegister`, `RFIRegister`, `SDRegister`, `IRRegister`, and `IF04/05/08/09List.jsx` were fixed to use the correct field names. **Still broken** in: `DARList`, `MRFList`, `ProcurementList`, `IF06List`, `IF07List`, `IF12List`, `DocumentRegister`, `DrawingRegister`, `MRFForm`, `DeliveryList`, `Dashboard`, `Topbar`. Same mechanical fix needed (`.code`→`.project_code`, `.name`→`.project_name`) whenever one of those is touched.
- **RLS is off** on every table except the 5 People & Access tables + `profiles` (re-enabled 2026-07-03 with policies keyed off a `current_user_role()` SQL helper — Admin/Planner write, broad authenticated read). Every other table still has RLS disabled with `GRANT ALL` to `anon`/`authenticated` — access control there is UI-level only, not enforced by the database. Re-enabling it project-wide was explicitly deferred by the user; raise it again next time a new module touches sensitive data.
- **New Supabase tables default to RLS-enabled with zero policies** on this project (confirmed empirically) — if a fresh `CREATE TABLE` mysteriously rejects every insert with `"new row violates row-level security policy"`, that's why. Either add policies or `ALTER TABLE ... DISABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` to match the rest of the app's current model.
- A `dist/` build-output folder is tracked in git despite being in `.gitignore` (force-added at some point in the past). Harmless — Vercel rebuilds from source and ignores it — but it's repo clutter if anyone wants to clean it up (`git rm -r --cached dist`).
- `xlsx` (used by `boqImportService.js`) is a real `package.json` dependency and installs fine on a clean `npm install` — if your local `node_modules` is missing it, just run `npm install`, it's not a real project problem.

## Deploy

Vercel project `aicc1/apcp`, production alias `https://apcp-nine.vercel.app`, auto-deploys on push to `main`. Pushes currently go out manually via GitHub Desktop.
